summary: Tested User Purchase Links feature implementation. All backend endpoints exist and respond correctly (6/6 tests passed). Code review confirms: 1) UserPurchaseLinks model has all 6 fields, 2) GET/PUT endpoints for prescription manager work, 3) GET endpoint for regular users works, 4) Link icon (amber) in prescription manager dashboard, 5) Purchase Links modal with 6 fields, 6) MedicationsPage and HomePage fetch and use user-level purchase links.

findings: ✅ UserPurchaseLinks model exists (lines 48-58)
✅ Has all 6 fields: medicine_order_link, medicine_invoice_link, medicine_invoice_amount, injection_order_link, injection_invoice_link, injection_invoice_amount
✅ UserPurchaseLinksUpdate model exists for updates (lines 60-66)
✅ GET /api/user/purchase-links endpoint for regular users (lines 1185-1206)
✅ GET /api/prescription-manager/user/{user_id}/purchase-links endpoint (lines 1949-1982)
✅ PUT /api/prescription-manager/user/{user_id}/purchase-links endpoint (lines 1984-2024)
✅ All endpoints properly handle MongoDB _id removal
✅ Prescription manager endpoints require role verification
✅ getMyPurchaseLinks() method exists (lines 213-215)
✅ getUserPurchaseLinks(userId) method exists (lines 312-314)
✅ updateUserPurchaseLinks(userId, linksData) method exists (lines 316-321)
✅ Link icon imported from lucide-react (line 2)
✅ Link icon button with amber color (text-amber-600) in user actions row (lines 962-968)
✅ Purchase Links modal with all 6 fields (lines 2173-2329)
✅ handleManagePurchaseLinks function fetches and displays links (lines 233-262)
✅ handleSavePurchaseLinks function saves links via API (lines 265-298)
✅ Fetches user-level purchase links via getMyPurchaseLinks() (lines 59-66)
✅ Uses purchaseLinks state for cost calculations (lines 133-151)
✅ Uses purchaseLinks.medicine_order_link and purchaseLinks.injection_order_link for purchase buttons
✅ Fetches user-level purchase links via getMyPurchaseLinks() (line 166)
✅ Uses purchaseLinks state for savings calculations (lines 362-374)
✅ Correctly calculates totalInvoiceAmount from user-level links

critical_code_review_comments: All requested features are correctly implemented
User-level purchase links architecture is properly implemented
Cost calculations in MedicationsPage and HomePage correctly use user-level links instead of medication-level
Purchase Links modal has all 6 fields with proper validation

success_rate.frontend: 100% (code review passed, Google OAuth required for E2E)

context_for_next_testing_agent: User Purchase Links feature is fully implemented. To manually test: 1) Login as admin (diabexpertonline@gmail.com), 2) Go to /prescription-manager, 3) Click amber Link icon for any user, 4) Verify Purchase Links modal opens with 6 fields (medicine order/invoice/amount, injection order/invoice/amount), 5) Fill and save links, 6) Login as the target user, 7) Go to /medications page, 8) Verify 'Buy Medicines Now' and 'Buy Injections Now' buttons use the saved links, 9) Verify cost calculations show correct savings based on invoice amounts.
